mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which systematically reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer demand and cheap financing, which jointly accelerate corporate revenue growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when RSAFS is high and FEDFUNDS is low, with a negative Rank IC over 2-year rolling windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum in equity markets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal shows no statistical edge with Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.03 during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic strength and rising capital expenditure, which jointly boost corporate profit forecasts and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise together, with a negative information coefficient over 3-year rolling windows, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction and falling aggregate demand, which systematically reduces corporate earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive during confirmed GDP contractions, or the signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.03 specifically within recessionary regimes.
